title: Smoky Crunchy Argentinian Tomatoes Empanadas
description: A lazy Sunday dish featuring plum tomatoes, beurre-monte, and a crispy empanada crust. This dairy-free friendly empanada is perfect for a weekend brunch.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Gradually add in the vegetable oil and egg replacer (or egg), mixing until dough forms. Divide dough into 8 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a separate bowl, mix together plum tomatoes, beurre-monte, apple cider vinegar, and chopped cilantro (if using).
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the tomato mixture onto one half of the dough circle. Fold the other half over the filling, pressing edges to seal.
Use a fork to crimp the edges of the empanada. Brush with a little bit of water.
Place empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each empanada.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
